label: SES-015 close-out — crm_candidate schema-design conversation (workstream conversation #4 of 4 — final)

session.title: crm_candidate schema design — methodology entity schema workstream conversation #4
session.summary: Fourth and final of four schema-design conversations in the methodology-entity-schema-design workstream. Produced the crm_candidate schema specification (PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/methodology-schema-specs/crm_candidate.md, 452 lines) defining the crm_candidate entity type for v2 storage under minimum-viable v0.4 scope, plus a companion artifact: the v0.4-build-planning kickoff prompt at PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/ui-PRD-v0.4-build-planning-kickoff.md (210 lines), which seeds the fifth and final workstream conversation. Five architectural questions resolved into five decisions (DEC-060 through DEC-064). Inherited and applied without re-decision all cross-spec conventions established and reaffirmed across SES-012 / SES-013 / SES-014 (parent-prefix field naming per DEC-046; {source}_{verb}_{target} relationship-kind naming per DEC-048; soft-3-letter prefix posture per DEC-044; engagement-global case-insensitive name uniqueness; standard 8-endpoint API surface; default ListDetailPanel UI under the Methodology sidebar group; decomposed reference handling; no archived status). Deviates from the rejection-via-soft-delete cross-spec principle (DEC-047, DEC-052) by introducing 'removed' as a first-class status value for mid-engagement candidate drops; deviation rationale in section 3.4.4 of the spec, following the deviation-rationale pattern process.md set in its section 3.4.1 (DEC-056). One new planning item authored (PI-012 — crm_candidate structured-metadata enums for v0.5+ pending CBM-redo signal). Acceptance criteria captured as 12 testable statements. Closes the per-entity schema-design portion of the workstream; the v0.4-build-planning conversation is queued against the kickoff this conversation also authored.
session.conversation_reference: Claude.ai schema-design conversation on 05-12-26 that produced PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/methodology-schema-specs/crm_candidate.md v1.0 and PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/ui-PRD-v0.4-build-planning-kickoff.md, both committed to main at 4d5863a. Fourth and final per-entity schema-design conversation in the methodology entity schema-design workstream established by SES-011. No transcript preserved per DEC-025.
session.topics_covered:
Seed prompt: "Design the `crm_candidate` entity type schema for v2's storage layer. A `crm_candidate` is one of the two or three CRM products selected at evolved Phase 1 for multi-deploy through the iteration loop — open source vs. commercial, hosting choices, budget, integrations, team-IT capabilities. The set persists across all iterations and is the basis for the final CRM selection in Phase 5. Drive a structured architectural discussion that produces two deliverables: `PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/methodology-schema-specs/crm_candidate.md` — the complete schema specification per the template in `methodology-entity-schema-spec-guide.md`; and a v0.4-build-planning kickoff prompt at `PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/ui-PRD-v0.4-build-planning-kickoff.md` that seeds the fifth and final workstream conversation. Fourth and final per-entity schema-design conversation; workstream's coda."

Pre-flight reading completed: crmbuilder/CLAUDE.md (universal entry, including v2 reference vocabulary mechanics at line 48 and direct-API identifier-computation note at line 52); methodology-schema-workstream-plan.md (master); methodology-entity-schema-spec-guide.md (template, section 6 cross-spec consistency requirements and section 7 validation gates); methodology-schema-specs/domain.md v1.0 (first predecessor; source of cross-spec conventions); methodology-schema-specs/entity.md v1.0 (second predecessor; decomposed reference handling, master-pane column deferral); methodology-schema-specs/process.md v1.0 (third predecessor; precedent for justified deviation from cross-spec norms); evolved-methodology-phase-outline.md sections 3 Phase 1 / Phase 3 / Phase 5 (Initial CRM Candidate Set output, persistence across iterations, CRM Selection Decision); db-export/sessions.json snapshots through SES-014 (current state at apply commit 2accd8c); db-export/decisions.json snapshots through DEC-059; crmbuilder-v2/src/crmbuilder_v2/access/vocab.py (current ENTITY_TYPES set, REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS, _kinds_for_pair rules).

Initial pre-flight finding: precondition not met. The schema-design-kickoff-crm_candidate.md predecessor list named domain / entity / process as all required complete before this conversation opens, but the initial repo pull showed only domain.md and entity.md on disk (HEAD at 83c108c). Surfaced this as a flow-stopper with two options: pause and run the process schema-design conversation first, or proceed anyway accepting the cross-spec consistency cost. User responded by running the process conversation in parallel and pushing the results; second pull surfaced process.md v1.0 plus the SES-013 / SES-014 close-out apply commit (HEAD at 2accd8c). Pre-flight resumed and completed with all three predecessors confirmed.

Architectural questions resolved in order:

Q1 — Identifier prefix. Adopted CRM (three letters) under domain.md's soft-3-letter posture; methodology context disambiguates against 'the CRM software' / 'CRM Builder' (the disambiguation supplied by usage context — identifier appearances in UI and methodology documents read as 'CRM-001 (EspoCRM)' or similar). Four-letter CRMC alternative rejected as adding a letter the context already supplies. Decided as routine and announced; no pushback. → DEC-060.

Q2 — Status lifecycle structure. Adopted Model A (four-status enum: active / selected / declined / removed) with three terminal states, an access-layer singleton-selected constraint, default starter 'active'. Deviates from the cross-spec rejection-via-soft-delete principle (DEC-047, DEC-052) by introducing 'removed' as a first-class status value for mid-engagement drops; deviation rationale in spec section 3.4.4. Model B (three-status lifecycle with drop-via-soft-delete) considered and rejected: methodology dynamics of consultant-driven lived-deployment adjustments are meaningfully different from authoring-error rejections, justifying the deviation. Soft-delete preserved for the authoring-error path (record created in error). Three terminal states reflect engagement-event semantics: reversing a Phase 5 outcome or a deliberate mid-engagement removal is a bigger methodology gesture than a status edit should faithfully represent. Singleton-selected constraint enforced at the access layer (HTTP 422 with selected_candidate_already_exists naming the existing record's identifier), matching v2's existing soft-FK pattern. Constraint applies on POST, PATCH/PUT, and restore. UX rough edge acknowledged and logged for CBM-redo-surface: recovering from a fat-fingered transition into 'selected' requires the soft-delete + restore-elsewhere path because terminal states have no transitions out. → DEC-062.

Q3 — Field inventory under minimum-viable v0.4 scope. Adopted Option A (thinnest). Five substantive fields: crm_candidate_identifier (server-assigned, CRM-NNN format), crm_candidate_name (required, engagement-global case-insensitive unique), crm_candidate_fit_reason (required plain text, one-paragraph rationale), crm_candidate_notes (optional consultant scratchpad, plain text), crm_candidate_status (required enum, default 'active' per Q2). No structured classification metadata in v0.4 (vendor_url, hosting_type, license_type, price_tier all deferred to v0.5+ under new PI-012). No storage-level length caps. Inherited timestamps (created_at, updated_at, deleted_at). Option B (Option A + vendor_url) and Option C (full kickoff inventory: vendor_url + three enums) considered and rejected on the minimum-viable principle (Phase 1 produces 2-3 candidates per engagement; structured fields' scannability gain is small relative to schema-UI-migration cost at that count; the Phase 1 selection criteria all live in fit_reason prose naturally; extraction to structured fields later is mechanical, deciding the right enum values is easier with real CBM-redo signal). Parent-prefix field naming inherited from DEC-046 without re-establishment. → DEC-061.

Q4 — Relationship posture and vocabulary additions. No outgoing relationships from crm_candidate in v0.4. No new relationship-kind vocabulary entries registered. Single mechanical addition: crm_candidate added to ENTITY_TYPES in vocab.py (alongside parallel additions of domain, entity, process from this v0.4 release), with Alembic migration extending refs.source_type and refs.target_type CHECK constraints to admit all four new methodology entity types. By virtue of being in ENTITY_TYPES, crm_candidate automatically participates in the universal kinds (is_about, references — valid for any pair), in decided_in (when target is session), and in supersedes (when both source and target are crm_candidate). Existing universal vocabulary covers the kickoff's anticipated 'cited_by_decision' and 'discussed_in_session' semantics without need for new kinds; kickoff's working names were imprecise and the correct names are the existing universal ones. v0.5+ anticipated kinds (deployment_log_targets_candidate, comparison_compares_candidates) deferred along with the entity types that would source them (PI-004 territory). Cross-spec consistency check verified clean: no naming collisions across the four specs' vocab additions. → DEC-063.

Q5 — API surface, UI defaults, singleton-selected enforcement, and acceptance criteria. Standard 8-endpoint set with no deviations from cross-spec defaults. Status-transition validation server-side per section 3.5.3. Singleton-selected enforcement at the access layer on POST / PATCH-PUT / restore per section 3.5.4. Default ListDetailPanel UI under the existing Methodology sidebar group at position #4 (completing the workstream-ordered set: domains, entities, processes, crm_candidates). Master pane columns: identifier / name / status / updated_at. Detail pane: identifier (read-only), name, fit_reason, notes (collapsed under 'Internal notes'), status, ReferencesSection (renders inbound governance-entity citations). Standard create / edit / delete dialogs with delete-dialog clarifying note distinguishing soft-delete (authoring-error path) from status='removed' (legitimate mid-engagement drop) — exact wording deferred to v0.4-build. Master-pane sort behavior for terminal-state records (interleave vs status-first) deferred to v0.4-build. 12 testable acceptance criteria including the singleton-selected enforcement, ENTITY_TYPES registration, and a sample CBM-redo Phase 1 + Phase 5 round-trip. → DEC-064.

Outline of both deliverables proposed and approved; end-to-end drafting executed without per-section confirmation. Spec and v0.4-build-planning kickoff committed to main at 4d5863a (662 insertions across two files). One new planning item authored: PI-012 (crm_candidate structured-metadata enums for v0.5+ pending CBM-redo signal).

Workstream-level closure: this conversation closes the per-entity schema-design portion of the methodology-entity-schema-design workstream. The workstream produced four schema specs (domain.md, entity.md, process.md, crm_candidate.md), nineteen decisions (DEC-044 through DEC-049 from SES-012; DEC-050 through DEC-054 from SES-013; DEC-055 through DEC-059 from SES-014; DEC-060 through DEC-064 from SES-015 pending apply), seven new planning items (PI-006 / PI-007 / PI-008 from SES-012; PI-009 / PI-010 from SES-013; PI-011 from SES-014; PI-012 from SES-015 pending apply), and the companion v0.4-build-planning kickoff. The successor v0.4-build-planning conversation integrates the four specs into the v0.4 release artifacts (PRD, implementation plan, slice build prompts) starting with the cross-spec consistency check per spec guide section 7.2.
session.artifacts_produced:
PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/methodology-schema-specs/crm_candidate.md (the crm_candidate methodology entity schema specification, v1.0, 452 lines). Fourth and final spec in the methodology-schema-specs/ directory introduced by SES-012.

PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/ui-PRD-v0.4-build-planning-kickoff.md (the seed prompt for the fifth and final workstream conversation, v1.0, 210 lines). Authored alongside the schema spec per the schema-design kickoff's instructions for this fourth conversation.

Five decisions:
- DEC-060: crm_candidate identifier prefix and format (CRM, three letters)
- DEC-061: crm_candidate field inventory under minimum-viable v0.4 scope (Option A — thinnest)
- DEC-062: crm_candidate lifecycle, four-status enum with three terminal states, singleton-selected constraint, and removed-status deviation from rejection-via-soft-delete
- DEC-063: crm_candidate relationship posture and ENTITY_TYPES expansion (no new vocab kinds)
- DEC-064: crm_candidate API surface, UI defaults, singleton-selected enforcement, and acceptance criteria for v0.4 (12 testable statements)

One new planning item:
- PI-012: crm_candidate structured-metadata enums (vendor_url, hosting_type, license_type, price_tier) for v0.5+ pending CBM-redo signal

This session record (SES-015).

Five decided_in references (DEC-060..DEC-064 → SES-015).

Git commits on crmbuilder main: 4d5863a (crm_candidate.md v1.0 + ui-PRD-v0.4-build-planning-kickoff.md initial draft, both committed and ready for Doug to push). Close-out payload commit forthcoming with this JSON file and the companion apply prompt.
session.in_flight_at_end:
Workstream's four schema-design conversations are complete. The methodology-entity-schema-design workstream's per-entity design portion is finished; the workstream's planning portion remains in one final conversation.

Next workstream conversation: v0.4-build-planning. Kickoff at PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/ui-PRD-v0.4-build-planning-kickoff.md (authored by this conversation alongside the crm_candidate spec). The build-planning conversation takes all four schema specs (domain.md, entity.md, process.md, crm_candidate.md) as input and produces the v0.4 release artifacts:
- v0.4 PRD at PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/ui-PRD-v0.4.md
- v0.4 implementation plan at PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/ui-v0.4-implementation-plan.md
- Slice build prompts at PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-v2-ui-v0.4-{A..*}-*.md
- Spec guide section 6 amendment reflecting parent-prefix field naming and {source}_{verb}_{target} relationship-kind naming conventions established by SES-012

First task in the build-planning conversation: cross-spec consistency check per spec guide section 7.2. Two documented deviations to validate (both well-justified in their source specs): process's no-status-field deviation (DEC-056, process.md section 3.4.1) and crm_candidate's removed-status deviation (DEC-062, crm_candidate.md section 3.4.4). Visual inspection during SES-015 confirmed no naming collisions across the four specs' vocabulary additions; the build-planning conversation re-verifies as a formal gate.

Open questions accumulated across the workstream that the v0.4-build-planning conversation settles:
1. Slice breakdown (working assumption: hybrid 6-slice pattern — foundation, then one per entity-type, then close-out).
2. Migration ordering across the four new methodology entity types' tables and the references CHECK constraint extensions.
3. Coordinated affiliation/handoff create-dialog flow (entity-to-domain affiliation per DEC-053; process-to-process handoff per DEC-058) — both deferred from the source-side specs with the suggestion that both panels behave consistently.
4. PI-006 sizing — governance-entity field-name retrofit in v0.4 scope or v0.5+ (carries from SES-012; working assumption: v0.5+).
5. PI-008 sizing — in-app inbox folder watcher in v0.4 scope or v0.5+ (carries from SES-012; working assumption: v0.5+).
6. Spec guide section 6 amendment scope (forward-only for methodology; governance unchanged).
7. Master-pane sort behavior for terminal-state crm_candidate records (carries from this conversation).
8. Delete-dialog clarifying note wording for crm_candidate (carries from this conversation).
9. Concurrent identifier-assignment mechanism for all four new entity types (carries from all four schema-design conversations).

After the v0.4-build-planning conversation closes, the workstream's planning arc is complete and the build phase begins under Claude Code execution of the slice build prompts, followed eventually by a v0.4 build-closeout session record at v0.4 ship.

decisions.title: crm_candidate identifier prefix and format
decisions.context: The crm_candidate methodology entity needs an identifier prefix. Per domain.md section 3.1 (DEC-044), the soft-3-letter posture preserves flexibility for downstream methodology entity types to adopt 4-letter prefixes if the 3-letter form is ambiguous (process.md adopted PROC under this clause per DEC-055). The working assumption from the workstream plan (line 168) and from the schema-design-kickoff-crm_candidate.md document was CRM (three letters). Potential ambiguity worth considering: 'CRM' as a string also denotes 'the CRM software' broadly and 'CRM Builder' specifically.
decisions.decision: Adopt CRM as the identifier prefix for the crm_candidate methodology entity. Format CRM-NNN, zero-padded to 3 digits, server-assigned on POST omission with helper at GET /crm_candidates/next-identifier per DEC-043. Affirms the soft-3-letter posture established by DEC-044 without invoking the explicit deviation clause.
decisions.rationale: CRM reads cleanly in the methodology context: a CRM-NNN identifier names a candidate product (the kind of thing the engagement might deploy to). The disambiguation against 'the CRM software' or 'CRM Builder' is supplied by usage context — in the v2 UI and methodology documents, identifier appearances read as 'CRM-001 (EspoCRM)' or similar, with the human-readable name supplying immediate disambiguation. Three letters also adheres to the v2 governance-entity norm (DEC, SES, RSK, TOP, REF, CHR, STA — all three letters), maintains consistency with the other three workstream prefixes where their 3-letter form is unambiguous (DOM, ENT), and avoids adding a letter the methodology context already supplies.
decisions.alternatives_considered: CRMC (four letters — 'CRM Candidate'): rejected for adding a letter the methodology context already supplies; the kickoff and workstream plan both worked from CRM as the assumption and no disambiguation friction surfaced during the conversation. CC (two letters): rejected because the spec guide section 6 disallows prefixes under three letters, and collision risk would be substantial. CAND (four letters, 'candidate'): rejected as too generic — 'candidate' is the conceptual category shared with domain/entity candidate-statused records, not the entity name; the prefix should name what the entity IS, not its starter status.
decisions.consequences: crm_candidate.md section 3.1 documents the prefix and the rationale for affirming three letters rather than invoking the explicit deviation clause. No collision with existing prefixes (DEC, SES, RSK, PI, TOP, REF, CHR, STA, DOM, ENT, PROC). Identifier-asymmetry helper endpoint (GET /crm_candidates/next-identifier) ships alongside the standard 8-endpoint set per DEC-043. Workstream-level: CRM completes the four prefixes (DOM, ENT, PROC, CRM); all four ship together in v0.4 under the new Methodology sidebar group.

decisions.title: crm_candidate field inventory and validation under minimum-viable v0.4 scope
decisions.context: The crm_candidate methodology entity hosts Phase 1 Initial CRM Candidate Set members per the evolved-methodology phase outline: 2-3 CRM products per engagement, proposed by CRM Builder and verified by client, persisting across iterations as the input to Phase 3 multi-deploy. The schema-design-kickoff-crm_candidate.md document named a working minimum (identifier, name, fit_reason, status) plus four optional metadata fields (vendor_url as free text, hosting_type as cloud/self_hosted/both enum, license_type as open_source/commercial/freemium enum, price_tier as free/paid enum). The minimum-viable v0.4 scope philosophy demands the thinnest schema that faithfully hosts Phase 1's output. The choice was which subset of the optional metadata fields makes the v0.4 cut.
decisions.decision: Adopt Option A — thinnest. Five substantive fields: crm_candidate_identifier (server-assigned, CRM-NNN format, unique); crm_candidate_name (required plain text, non-empty trimmed, case-insensitive engagement-global unique); crm_candidate_fit_reason (required plain text, non-empty trimmed, one-paragraph Phase 1 selection-criteria rationale); crm_candidate_notes (optional plain text, consultant scratchpad); crm_candidate_status (required enum, default 'active' per DEC-062). No structured classification metadata in v0.4. No storage-level length caps. Parent-prefix field naming inherited from DEC-046 (all fields including identifier and timestamps adopt the crm_candidate_ prefix). Inherited timestamps (crm_candidate_created_at, _updated_at, _deleted_at).
decisions.rationale: Phase 1 produces 2-3 candidates per engagement, a count too small for scannability gains from structured enum fields to outweigh their schema-and-UI-and-migration cost. The Phase 1 selection criteria named in the phase outline (hosting / license / budget / integrations / team-IT) all live in crm_candidate_fit_reason prose naturally — a one-paragraph rationale covers them without forcing a particular enum-shape. The v0.4-deferred adjacent artifacts (Phase 3 deployment logs, Phase 4 Comparison Artifact) are the natural home for structured comparison data, and they don't exist in v0.4. Extracting structured fields from prose later is mechanical (add columns via Alembic migration, optional NULL on existing rows); deciding the right enum values is easier with real CBM-redo signal in hand than with speculation now. Case-insensitive engagement-global name uniqueness mirrors DEC-045 / DEC-051 / DEC-056 cross-spec precedent.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Option B (Option A plus crm_candidate_vendor_url — free-text URL field, no enum cost, no Alembic CHECK constraint, no UI combo): considered for its low marginal cost and consultant-utility (saves searches when needing vendor docs). Rejected on the same minimum-viable principle: vendor_url is not Phase-1-output content per the phase outline; if CBM-redo surfaces utility, a one-column migration in v0.5+ adds it cheaply. Option C (full kickoff inventory: vendor_url + three enums): rejected as speculative — three CHECK-constrained enums in v0.4 commit the schema to particular taxonomy choices before any real-engagement signal has informed them; each enum requires Alembic constraint, access-layer validation, UI combo, and an additional acceptance criterion. Option D variant (vendor_url + hosting_type + license_type, dropping price_tier as the coarsest): not surfaced as a separate option but implicitly handled by the same minimum-viable principle applied to Option C.
decisions.consequences: crm_candidate.md section 3.2 documents the five fields with full validation rules. PI-012 (new) tracks all four deferred metadata fields for v0.5+ pending CBM-redo signal, with explicit trigger conditions for pulling the PI to v0.5 ahead of other v0.5 candidates. crm_candidate ships as the workstream's smallest schema (domain has seven fields, entity has six, process has seven; crm_candidate has five plus three timestamps for eight total columns), appropriately reflecting its position as the simplest of the four methodology entities. Phase 1 selection-criteria information lives in fit_reason prose in v0.4; analytics across engagements scanning for hosting / license / budget would parse text rather than query structured columns — acceptable cost at the small engagement count and pending CBM-redo signal.

decisions.title: crm_candidate lifecycle: four-status enum, three terminal states, singleton-selected constraint, removed-status deviation from rejection-via-soft-delete
decisions.context: The crm_candidate methodology entity has an engagement-long lifecycle ending in a deliberate selection outcome at Phase 5 (the client picks one winning CRM from the candidate set). Mid-engagement, a candidate may also be pulled from the set based on lived deployment experience across iterations (team-IT capabilities don't match, vendor terms change, deployment friction reveals an unworkable fit). The cross-spec rejection-via-soft-delete principle established by DEC-047 and reaffirmed by DEC-052 says 'status values track engagement-scope lifecycle; soft-delete tracks existence-in-the-record.' For domain and entity, 'we proposed it and the client rejected it' is existence-in-the-record removal handled by soft-delete — the consultant got the proposal wrong. For crm_candidate, mid-engagement removal is consultant-driven adjustment based on lived deployment experience, not authoring-error rejection — a meaningfully different methodology gesture. The conversation evaluated whether to preserve the cross-spec principle (three-status enum: active / selected / declined, with mid-engagement drop via soft-delete) or to deviate (four-status enum including 'removed' as a first-class status value, with soft-delete reserved for the authoring-error path only).
decisions.decision: Adopt Model A: four-status enum (active / selected / declined / removed) with three terminal states. Default starter status 'active' (records exist in v2 only after CRM Builder's Phase 1 proposal has been verified by the client; no propose-verify pre-state). Transition map: active → {selected, declined, removed}; all three non-active states are terminal with no transitions out, returning HTTP 422 with invalid_status_transition on any out-transition attempt. Access-layer singleton-selected constraint: at most one live (non-soft-deleted) crm_candidate per engagement may hold status='selected'; enforced on POST (creating selected when one exists), PATCH/PUT (transitioning into selected), and POST /restore (restoring a selected record when another live selected exists), returning HTTP 422 with selected_candidate_already_exists and the existing record's identifier in the error body. Deviation from cross-spec rejection-via-soft-delete principle (DEC-047 / DEC-052): 'removed' is a first-class status value, not soft-delete. Soft-delete preserved for the authoring-error path only (record created in error — wrong product name, accidentally-added duplicate, etc.); the cross-spec principle survives intact for that path. Deviation rationale documented at length in crm_candidate.md section 3.4.4, following the deviation-rationale pattern that process.md set with its own no-status-field deviation in section 3.4.1 (DEC-056).
decisions.rationale: Three rationales. (1) For 'removed' as a first-class status rather than soft-delete: a mid-engagement-removed candidate was legitimately in the set, was lived-deployed-to during some iterations, and was correctly pulled before Phase 5 based on real deployment signal. That history carries methodology value — Phase 5 selection rationale may cite what was considered and rejected, governance decisions documenting the removal reference the candidate, retrospectives across engagements scan past candidate sets. Demoting such records to soft-deleted-and-hidden mis-frames legitimate methodology gestures as errors. The dynamics differ enough from domain / entity / process (where rejection is client-driven verification of a consultant proposal) to justify the deviation, and the deviation is small in scope (one extra enum value, one deviation-rationale subsection, no new infrastructure). (2) For terminal states with no transitions out: each non-active value represents a real engagement event (Phase 5 outcome or deliberate mid-engagement removal). Reversing such an event after the fact is a bigger methodology gesture than a status edit can faithfully represent; if revision is needed (e.g., the client changes their selected CRM after closure), a fresh governance decision record and explicit administrative action are the correct mechanism. (3) For the singleton-selected constraint at the access layer (not DB-level): Phase 5 picks one winner; multiple selected records would be ambiguous about which CRM is the engagement's chosen platform. Access-layer enforcement matches v2's existing soft-FK pattern (FKs validated at the access layer per DEC-006, not enforced by SQLite) and keeps the constraint relaxable in future schemas (e.g., a hypothetical multi-winner methodology variant would only need access-layer logic changes, not a schema migration).
decisions.alternatives_considered: Model B — three-status enum (active / selected / declined) with mid-engagement drop via soft-delete, preserving the cross-spec rejection-via-soft-delete principle unchanged. Considered and rejected on the methodology-dynamics-differ argument above. Database-level CHECK constraint enforcing singleton-selected (e.g., a partial unique index where status='selected'): rejected because v2's integrity discipline places constraints at the access layer per DEC-006; the access-layer pattern keeps the constraint logic in one place and consistent with existing entity-type behavior, and keeps the constraint relaxable without a schema migration. Allowing transitions out of terminal states (e.g., selected → active for selection-decision reversal): rejected as a status-edit responsibility too large for a single field; if needed in practice the v0.5+ mitigation is a dedicated reverse-Phase-5-outcome administrative path rather than relaxing the terminal-state rule. Propose-verify pre-state (e.g., 'candidate' starter analogous to domain / entity): rejected because Phase 1 candidate-set proposal is a lightweight gesture (the consultant names two or three products and the client agrees in the same conversation), unlike domain and entity proposals which involve substantive back-and-forth — the verification event IS the record-creation moment in practice.
decisions.consequences: crm_candidate.md section 3.4 documents the lifecycle at length, including sections 3.4.1 (status values table), 3.4.2 (transition semantics), 3.4.3 (singleton-selected constraint), 3.4.4 (deviation rationale for 'removed' status), 3.4.5 (rejection-via-soft-delete preserved for the authoring-error path), 3.4.6 (no archived status), and 3.4.7 (soft-delete semantics). The cross-spec consistency check at the v0.4-build-planning conversation now has two documented deviations to validate: process's no-status-field deviation (DEC-056, process.md section 3.4.1) and crm_candidate's removed-status deviation (this DEC, crm_candidate.md section 3.4.4). Both are well-justified by methodology semantics and small in scope. The singleton-selected access-layer validator extends the standard transition-validation pattern from domain / entity / process; implementation cost is small. UX rough edge acknowledged and logged as a CBM-redo-surfaceable concern: recovering from a fat-fingered transition into 'selected' requires the soft-delete + restore-elsewhere path (because terminal states have no transitions out). If lived signal surfaces friction, a v0.5+ mitigation either relaxes the terminal-state rule for the selected→active direction or introduces an administrative reversal path.

decisions.title: crm_candidate relationship posture, ENTITY_TYPES expansion, and use of existing universal references vocabulary
decisions.context: The crm_candidate methodology entity does not relate to domain, entity, or process — CRM candidates are metadata about WHERE the iteration deploys, not about WHAT the iteration models. The two sides of v0.4's methodology schema (the what side: domain / entity / process; the where side: crm_candidate) are intentionally orthogonal. The schema-design-kickoff-crm_candidate.md document anticipated that governance entities (decisions, sessions) would need to reference crm_candidate records (for Phase 5 selection-decision capture, session records of candidate-set discussions, audit trails of mid-engagement candidate-set adjustments) and asked whether new relationship-kind values would be needed — naming 'cited_by_decision' and 'discussed_in_session' as working terms. The vocab.py inspection during pre-flight confirmed that the existing universal references vocabulary (is_about and references, valid for any pair) and the existing decided_in kind (valid when target is session) already cover those semantics.
decisions.decision: No outgoing relationships from crm_candidate in v0.4. No new relationship-kind values registered in REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS. Single mechanical addition: crm_candidate added to ENTITY_TYPES in crmbuilder-v2/src/crmbuilder_v2/access/vocab.py, alongside the parallel additions of domain, entity, and process from this v0.4 release. The Alembic migration extends refs.source_type and refs.target_type CHECK constraints to admit all four new methodology entity types in a single coordinated migration. By virtue of being in ENTITY_TYPES, crm_candidate automatically participates in the universal kinds (is_about, references — valid for any pair per the _kinds_for_pair generic clause), in decided_in (when source is crm_candidate and target is session, per the target=session rule), and in supersedes (when both source and target are crm_candidate, per the source_type==target_type rule). Inbound governance-entity citations (decisions, sessions citing crm_candidate) work via the existing universal vocabulary without further additions. No spec changes to the v0.3 DEC-033 cascading reference-create dialog are required — it reads RELATIONSHIP_RULES at render time and adapts automatically once vocab.py is updated.
decisions.rationale: The existing universal references vocabulary in vocab.py is rich enough to cover the kickoff's anticipated use cases: 'cited_by_decision' is covered by is_about / references from a decision source (renders inbound on crm_candidate as 'decided_in DEC-NNN' equivalent meaning); 'discussed_in_session' is covered by is_about from a session source or decided_in to a session target. The kickoff's working names were imprecise; the correct names are the existing universal ones. Phase 5 selection-decision capture lives in a governance decision record (DEC-NNN) with is_about edges to the chosen crm_candidate; no new vocabulary is needed for that pattern. Adding redundant relationship kinds would proliferate vocabulary without adding methodology expressiveness — exactly the kind of vocabulary bloat the cross-spec principle '{source}_{verb}_{target}' naming (DEC-048) and the careful gate on REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS additions (DEC-006) exist to prevent. The v0.5+ anticipated kinds (deployment_log_targets_candidate from Phase 3 deployment-log artifacts, comparison_compares_candidates from Phase 4 Comparison Artifacts) are deferred along with the entity types that would source them (PI-004 territory: the deployment_log and comparison_artifact entity types are themselves deferred to v0.5+).
decisions.alternatives_considered: Registering new vocabulary kinds with names like cited_by_decision (source=decision, target=crm_candidate) and discussed_in_session (source=crm_candidate or session, target=session or crm_candidate): rejected because the universal kinds already cover the semantics and the new kinds would either duplicate existing meaning (is_about does the work) or introduce ambiguous naming (which direction is 'discussed in'?). Pre-registering the v0.5+ kinds (deployment_log_targets_candidate, comparison_compares_candidates) in v0.4: rejected because their source entity types (deployment_log, comparison_artifact) don't exist in v0.4 — registering kinds for hypothetical source types creates dead vocab entries with no real validation semantics.
decisions.consequences: crm_candidate.md section 3.3 documents the relationship posture (3.3.1 outgoing — none, plus the ENTITY_TYPES expansion as the sole mechanical addition; 3.3.2 inbound anticipated via existing universal vocabulary plus informational v0.5+ table; 3.3.3 cross-spec naming convention adopted trivially with no new kinds registered; 3.3.4 hierarchy — not used). The v0.4 build's vocab.py change is one ENTITY_TYPES expansion (adding crm_candidate alongside domain, entity, process). The Alembic migration extends refs.source_type and refs.target_type CHECK constraints; one migration covers all four new entity types. The v0.3 references-create dialog adapts automatically. Cross-spec consistency check at v0.4-build-planning validates that the four specs' vocabulary additions do not conflict (visual inspection during this conversation confirmed clean: only entity.md and process.md register new kinds — entity_scopes_to_domain and process_hands_off_to_process respectively — and the names don't collide).

decisions.title: crm_candidate API surface, UI defaults, singleton-selected enforcement, and acceptance criteria for v0.4
decisions.context: Per cross-spec conventions from DEC-049 / DEC-054 / DEC-059, methodology entity types ship with a standard 8-endpoint API surface (GET list, GET single, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, POST /restore, GET /next-identifier per DEC-043) and default ListDetailPanel UI under the Methodology sidebar group introduced by domain.md. The crm_candidate spec required confirming the standard set with no deviations, settling the singleton-selected enforcement details from DEC-062 at the API layer, drafting UI specifics for the new panel, and producing testable acceptance criteria.
decisions.decision: Standard 8-endpoint API surface with no deviations from cross-spec defaults. Server-side status-transition validation per crm_candidate.md section 3.5.3 returning HTTP 422 with invalid_status_transition error body. Singleton-selected enforcement at the access layer on POST (creating with status='selected' when another live selected exists), PATCH/PUT (transitioning into selected when another live selected exists), and POST /restore (restoring a selected record when another live selected exists), returning HTTP 422 with {"error": "selected_candidate_already_exists", "existing": "CRM-NNN"} per crm_candidate.md section 3.5.4. Default ListDetailPanel UI under the existing Methodology sidebar group at position #4 (completing the workstream-ordered set: Domains, Entities, Processes, CRM Candidates — all four ship together in v0.4). Master pane columns: identifier / name / status / updated_at; default sort by identifier ascending. Detail pane in section-3.2 order: identifier (read-only label), name (single-line editor), fit_reason (multi-line editor with placeholder), notes (multi-line editor collapsed under 'Internal notes' header), status (combo box restricting choices to valid successors of current status), ReferencesSection widget (renders inbound governance-entity citations; outgoing-references list empty in v0.4). Standard create / edit / delete dialogs; delete-dialog includes a clarifying note distinguishing soft-delete (authoring-error path) from status='removed' (legitimate mid-engagement drop). Twelve testable acceptance criteria covering schema migration, identifier format constraint, engagement-global case-insensitive name uniqueness, status enum and transition validation, singleton-selected enforcement, access-layer method signatures, REST endpoint round-trip, soft-delete/restore for the authoring-error path, ENTITY_TYPES registration and references-table CHECK constraint extension, sidebar entry placement, master pane columns and sort, and end-to-end CBM-redo Phase 1 + Phase 5 round-trip through the UI. Master-pane sort behavior for terminal-state records (interleave vs status-first) and exact delete-dialog clarifying-note wording deferred to v0.4-build as small UI questions, not architectural choices.
decisions.rationale: The cross-spec API and UI defaults are well-established by three predecessor specs (domain, entity, process); no methodology-specific need to deviate. Singleton-selected enforcement at the access layer matches v2's existing soft-FK and status-transition validation patterns (HTTP 422 with structured error body), keeping the constraint logic uniform across methodology entity types and consistent with existing entity-type behavior. The constraint applies on all three paths (POST, PATCH/PUT, restore) because each can introduce a second selected record into the live set — handling all three uniformly through the same validator simplifies implementation. The ReferencesSection widget existing in v0.3 renders inbound governance-entity citations for free once ENTITY_TYPES is updated; no panel-specific UI infrastructure is needed for the inbound-references case. Sidebar position #4 follows workstream ordering established by the four schema-design kickoffs and confirmed in each predecessor spec's section 3.6.1. Twelve acceptance criteria balances coverage (matching the predecessor counts: domain 14, entity 16, process 15, crm_candidate 12 — reflecting crm_candidate's simpler relationship posture and absence of new vocabulary kinds) against minimum-viable test scope (each criterion is testable in the v0.4 build, not in the schema-design conversation).
decisions.alternatives_considered: Database-level CHECK constraint or partial unique index enforcing singleton-selected (e.g., 'UNIQUE INDEX ON crm_candidates (status) WHERE status=selected AND deleted_at IS NULL'): rejected because v2's soft-FK and status-transition disciplines place integrity at the access layer per DEC-006; matching that pattern keeps the constraint logic uniform and consistent with existing entity-type behavior, and keeps the constraint relaxable without a schema migration in future methodology variants. Inline reference-attachment in create / edit dialog bodies (e.g., POST /crm_candidates with embedded references payload): rejected per the cross-spec decomposed reference handling pattern (DEC-053, DEC-058); crm_candidate has no outgoing references in v0.4 anyway, making the question moot in practice. Master-pane Domains-style column for status grouping or affiliation rendering: not applicable here (crm_candidate has no domain affiliation per the orthogonal-sides-of-methodology-schema posture); the corresponding question for crm_candidate is master-pane sort behavior for terminal-state records, deferred to v0.4-build as a small UX question.
decisions.consequences: crm_candidate.md sections 3.5 (API surface), 3.6 (UI considerations), 3.7 (twelve acceptance criteria) document the implementation requirements. v0.4-build-planning conversation translates the 12 acceptance criteria into specific test cases for the build slice covering crm_candidate, and decides the two deferred UI questions (master-pane sort behavior, delete-dialog clarifying-note wording) along with the parallel deferred questions from entity.md and process.md (create-dialog affiliation/handoff flows). The full v0.4 release ships all four methodology entity panels together as a single visible-to-user unit under the new Methodology sidebar group, completing the workstream's deliverables.

planning_items.title: crm_candidate structured-metadata enums (vendor_url, hosting_type, license_type, price_tier) for v0.5+
planning_items.description:
DEC-061 adopted Option A (thinnest) for the crm_candidate field inventory under minimum-viable v0.4 scope. Four optional metadata fields named in the schema-design-kickoff-crm_candidate.md document were deferred from v0.4 to v0.5+ under this planning item:

- crm_candidate_vendor_url — optional free-text URL field pointing to the vendor's product or documentation page; no enum constraint, light URL-shape validation only.
- crm_candidate_hosting_type — optional enum: 'cloud' | 'self_hosted' | 'both'. Captures the Phase 1 hosting selection criterion as a structured value.
- crm_candidate_license_type — optional enum: 'open_source' | 'commercial' | 'freemium'. Captures the Phase 1 license/cost-model selection criterion as a structured value.
- crm_candidate_price_tier — optional enum: 'free' | 'paid'. Coarse capture of the Phase 1 budget selection criterion.

The deferral rationale is the minimum-viable v0.4 scope principle plus the small per-engagement candidate count (2-3 records typically), which makes scannability gains from structured fields small relative to their schema-and-UI-and-migration cost. The Phase 1 selection criteria all live in crm_candidate_fit_reason prose in v0.4.

Triggers to pull this PI to v0.5 ahead of other v0.5 candidates:
- CBM-redo reveals that consultants miss scannable classification at the master-pane level when reviewing the candidate set across iterations (the CBM-redo-friction trigger pattern established by DEC-042 for PI-001).
- Phase 3 deployment-log entity type or Phase 4 Comparison Artifact entity type (both deferred to v0.5+ entity types under PI-004 expansion) need to relate to structured candidate metadata for cross-iteration analysis.
- Multi-engagement analytics emerge as a v2 use case where parsing crm_candidate_fit_reason prose for hosting / license / budget across engagements is infeasible.

Implementation scope when pulled:
- Alembic migration extending the crm_candidates table with four optional new columns: vendor_url TEXT (nullable, no CHECK constraint), hosting_type TEXT (nullable, CHECK constraint on the three-value enum), license_type TEXT (nullable, CHECK constraint on the three-value enum), price_tier TEXT (nullable, CHECK constraint on the two-value enum). All new columns are nullable; existing crm_candidate records persist with the new columns NULL.
- Access-layer validators for the three enum fields (the URL field gets light client-side and access-layer URL-shape validation; the enums match the standard pattern used by domain_status, entity_status, process_classification).
- vocab.py expansion: not needed; the enums are non-reference values stored directly in the entity table.
- UI work: combo boxes for the three enums in the create/edit dialog and detail pane; free-text URL editor for vendor_url; possibly a master-pane column extension to render one or more of the new classification fields as scannable badges. Specific UI shape decided at v0.5+ planning time.
- Acceptance criteria additions for the four new fields: format validation, NULL-on-existing-records, transition validation if any enum values are constrained to transition rules (working assumption: no — classification fields here describe stable product attributes, not lifecycle states).

No backward-incompatible changes. The crm_candidate API surface stays at the standard 8 endpoints; the new fields are accepted as optional in POST / PATCH / PUT bodies and surfaced in GET responses.
